hero.headline: Debug everything in your PHP app.
hero.subheadline: One docker run. Exceptions, logs, dumps, emails, SMS and profiling — all in one real-time UI. Works with Sentry SDK, VarDumper, Monolog, XHProf, SMTP and SMS providers you already have. No cloud account. No code changes.

cleanApp.headline: Your app doesn't know we exist.
cleanApp.body: Buggregator runs beside your application as a standalone Docker container. Your codebase doesn't change. Your dependencies don't change. You already have the SDKs installed — they just need a different address.

noDeps.title: No new dependencies
noDeps.body: Your app's composer.json stays untouched. No service providers. No migrations. No Buggregator code inside your project.

oneEnvVar.title: One env variable
oneEnvVar.body: Already using Sentry SDK, VarDumper, or Monolog? Change where they send data. That's it. Your code doesn't need to know about Buggregator.

crossProject.title: One instance, all your apps
crossProject.body: Run one Buggregator container. Point all your projects at it. Microservices, monorepos, multiple teams — one dashboard.

devEnv.headline: Made for dev environments.
devEnv.subheadline: No account. No credit card. No project setup.
devEnv.body: Add Buggregator to your docker-compose.yml alongside your other services. Change your .env. Start debugging. That's 60 seconds — not 15 minutes of cloud dashboard configuration.

cloudSetup.label: Getting started with Sentry / Mailtrap / Blackfire:
steps: Wait for first event to arrive
cloudSetup.footer: ~15 minutes before you see a single debug event

devEnv.composeCaption: No environment configuration needed. Works out of the box.
devEnv.privacy: Your debug data never leaves your machine. No cloud storage. No third-party servers. Full privacy.

envComparison.title: Just change the address
envComparison.sentryAnnotation: Only the host changed. Your Sentry SDK keeps working exactly the same.

table.title: How does it compare?
table.disclaimer: Telescope and Ray are excellent tools for their use cases. This comparison highlights the architectural difference: Buggregator is infrastructure, not a library.

replaces.title: Replaces your local dev stack — for free
replaces.footer: All of this. One command. Zero cost.

run.title: Run one command
run.description: Start Buggregator with a single docker run. No installation, no registration, no configuration files.

connect.title: Add one env variable
connect.description: Change one line in your .env file. Your existing Sentry SDK, VarDumper, Monolog, or Ray — they all just work.

open.title: Open your browser
open.description: Navigate to http://127.0.0.1:8000 and Buggregator is ready. No login required.

everything.title: See everything in one place
everything.description: Exceptions, logs, dumps, emails, profiling, HTTP requests — all in real time, all in one tab.

showcase.title: Everything in one dashboard
showcase.subtitle: 9 debugging tools. One interface.

descriptions.exceptions: Catch unhandled exceptions with full stack traces via Sentry SDK. See exactly where your code breaks.
descriptions.profiler: Visualize XHProf call graphs to find performance bottlenecks. Click any node to trace the path from entry point.
descriptions.vardump: Inspect variables with dump(). See object properties, array contents, and types — right in the browser.
descriptions.ray: Debug with ray() — tables, queries, models, jobs, mail, counters and 18 more payload types. Works with PHP, JS, Ruby, Go SDKs.
descriptions.emails: Intercept outgoing emails during development. Preview HTML, inspect headers — no emails actually sent.
descriptions.logs: Aggregate Monolog, Symfony, and Laravel logs in real time. Filter by level, search by message.
descriptions.http: Capture and inspect incoming HTTP requests. See headers, body, and query parameters at a glance.
descriptions.inspector: Monitor application performance with transaction tracing, memory tracking, and timeline breakdowns. Local APM alternative.
descriptions.sms: Intercept outgoing SMS during development. Auto-detects 40+ providers including Twilio, Vonage, Plivo, Sinch, and more.

showcase.profilerCaption: XHProf Call Graph · Laravel app · 891ms wall time

install.title: Get started in 60 seconds
install.trapNote: No Docker needed. Trap runs as a PHP CLI tool in your project.

demo.headline: Experience Buggregator in Action
demo.subtitle: Click any button to send a real event to a live Buggregator instance. Watch it appear in the UI on the left — exactly as it would in your own project.
demo.mobileBody: The interactive demo is best experienced on a larger screen. Visit buggregator.dev on your laptop or desktop to try it live.

frameworks.title: Works with your framework
frameworks.subtitle: No vendor lock-in. Your existing SDK just works.
frameworks.also: Also: JavaScript, Python, Ruby — via Sentry SDK

advanced.title: Ready for production teams
advanced.subtitle: Enterprise features when you need them.

sso.title: SSO Authentication
sso.desc: Auth0 & Kinde support for team environments

k8s.title: Kubernetes Ready
k8s.desc: Deploy as Deployment + Service in your cluster

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.desc: Trigger external actions on incoming events

multiproject.title: Multi-project Support
multiproject.desc: Separate events by project or team

trap.title: No Docker? No problem.
trap.description: Trap is a lightweight PHP CLI alternative. Install it as a composer package — no containers, no configuration.

jetbrains.title: Never leave your IDE
jetbrains.description: The Buggregator plugin for PhpStorm brings dumps, logs, and debug data directly into your editor.

community.title: Open source. Built with the community.
community.description: Buggregator is free forever. No paid plans, no feature gates. If it helps you, consider starring the repo.

sponsors.headline: Backed by the community
sponsors.subheadline: Buggregator is free forever. Sponsors make that possible.
sponsors.empty: Be the first to support Buggregator.

page.title: Support Buggregator
page.subheadline: Buggregator is and will always be free. No paid tiers, no feature gates. Sponsors help fund server costs, development time, and new features.

free.title: Keep it free
free.body: Sponsorship covers hosting, CI, and development time to ship new features.

recognition.title: Get recognized
recognition.body: Your logo on the homepage, README, and docs. Seen by PHP developers daily.

oss.title: Support open source
oss.body: Buggregator saves dev teams hours of setup every week. Give back.

page.noSponsors: No sponsors yet. Be the first!

contact.title: Want to become a company sponsor?
contact.body: Send us an email with your company name, logo, and website. We'll get back to you within 48 hours.
contact.or: or join developers supporting Buggregator on Patreon:
